items.headline: Uber and Lucid team up to bring robotaxis to Houston
items.summary: Yahoo Finance Senior Autos Reporter Pras Subramanian joins the show to discuss why Uber (UBER) and Lucid (LCID) chose Houston, Texas, as their next robotaxi market, what makes the city an attractive testing ground for autonomous ride-hailing and why there's so much riding on this launch for both companies.
items.why_it_matters: News impact is unclear; monitor price and volume reaction.

items.headline: Uber Robo-Taxis Move Step Closer as Self-Driving War Revs Up
items.summary: On Wednesday, ride-hailing giant  Uber  EV maker  Lucid  and self-driving technology start-up Nuro announced plans to launch robo-taxis in Houston in 2027.  Uber operates the network, Lucid builds the cars, and Nuro provides the autonomous driving technology.  The move is yet another example that the long-promised era of driverless cars has arrived.
items.why_it_matters: News impact is unclear; monitor price and volume reaction.

items.headline: SpaceX Shares Fall for First Time Since Blockbuster Debut
items.summary: (Bloomberg) -- SpaceX shares declined for the first time since its record initial public offering, snapping a three-day rally that had reached nearly 50%. items.headline: China’s Brutal Auto Price War Claims Its Biggest Victim Yet: BMW
items.summary: BMW  stock fell on Wednesday, dragging down shares of other European auto makers after a shocking cut to guidance that boils down to a weakening Chinese auto market.  The drops came after BMW cut its 2026 automotive profit margin guidance to 1% to 3%, down from 4% to 6%.  “BMW blamed the sharp cut to guidance on an acceleration in the negative development of the China passenger car market in [the second quarter], which had triggered a more intense competitive environment in China and in other countries in the Asia Pacific region,” wrote Bernstein analyst Stephen Reitman on Wednesday.
items.why_it_matters: News may increase risk, valuation pressure, or negative sentiment.

items.headline: Tesla Stock Is Down. An Argentina Charger Deal Won’t Help.
items.summary: Tesla  stock fell Wednesday.  The move came despite Argentina’s energy company,  YPF  and Tesla signing a memorandum of understanding to explore the development of a fast-charger network.  “The partnership tries to leverage on YPF’s leading fuel marketing operation in Argentina, with 55% of stations, the largest network in the country and the only with national scale,” Citi analyst Andres Cardona wrote on Tuesday.
items.why_it_matters: News impact is unclear; monitor price and volume reaction.
